title: Hapalostephium alpestre

It belongs to the genus Hapalostephium, which is a group of plants native to the western United States. The plant's classification is further divided into the family Asteraceae, order Asterales, and class Magnoliopsida. intro: Hapalostephium alpestre, also known as alpine fleabane, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hapalostephium alpestre include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
